title: Chewy Brazilian Coconut Dumplings
description: Nutty, chewy dumplings infused with bright orange juice and high‑protein coconut flavor, perfect for midnight cravings.

ingredients.name: chia seeds
ingredients.notes: mixed with 3 tbsp water to form gel

instructions: In a small bowl, combine the chia seeds with 3 tbsp water; let sit 5 min to form a gel.
In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the cooked rice, coconut flour, shredded coconut, protein powder, and sea salt.
Add the almond butter, maple syrup, vanilla extract, and orange juice to the dry mix; blend until a sticky dough forms.
Fold in the chia seed gel until fully incorporated; the dough should be pliable but firm.
Cover and refrigerate the dough for 20 minutes to firm up.
While the dough is resting, heat 1 tbsp coconut oil in a nonstick skillet over medium heat (about 350°F / 175°C).
Remove the dough from the fridge and divide it into 8 equal portions; roll each into a ball about 1.5 inches diameter.
Gently flatten each ball into a disc and insert a small lump of coconut oil in the center, then quickly seal the edges to encase the oil, forming a filled dumpling.
Fry the dumplings in batches, turning once, until golden brown and puffed, about 2–3 minutes per side.
Remove from skillet and drain on paper towels; serve warm, optionally dusted with powdered sugar or fresh orange zest for extra brightness.
